# Spikes

> Spikes is a feedback tool for AI-assisted building. Click elements, leave comments, get exact CSS selectors your AI agent can act on. Structured JSON output for Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI coding assistants.

## Key Concepts

- **Widget**: Drop-in JavaScript (`<script src="https://spikes.sh/widget.js">`) that adds a feedback button to any HTML page
- **CLI**: Rust binary for querying feedback, injecting widget, serving locally
- **Feedback format**: JSON with selector, rating (love/like/meh/no), comments, reviewer, timestamp
- **Two workflows**: (1) You reviewing agent work, (2) Collecting feedback from others

## Docs

- [Documentation](https://spikes.sh/docs.html.md): Complete CLI reference, widget configuration, data formats
- [Quick Start](https://spikes.sh/docs.html.md#quick-start): Get running in 30 seconds

## CLI Commands

- `spikes inject <dir>`: Add widget to HTML files
- `spikes serve`: Start local dev server (default port 3847)
- `spikes list`: List all feedback (supports --json, --rating, --page filters)
- `spikes list --json`: Machine-readable output for AI agents
- `spikes hotspots`: Find most-spiked elements
- `spikes export --format json`: Export all feedback
- `spikes mcp serve`: Start MCP server (stdio transport, local data)
- `spikes mcp serve --remote`: Start MCP server using hosted API
- `spikes mcp serve --transport http --port 3848`: Start MCP server over HTTP
- `spikes auth create-key`: Generate an API key for agent access
- `spikes auth list-keys`: List your API keys
- `spikes auth revoke-key <key_id>`: Revoke an API key

## MCP Tools

Spikes exposes 9 MCP tools via `spikes mcp serve`. These tools let AI agents read, write, and manage feedback programmatically.

### get_spikes
Get all feedback spikes with optional filters.
- `page` (String, optional): Filter by page name (e.g., 'index.html')
- `rating` (String, optional): Filter by rating: love, like, meh, or no
- `unresolved_only` (bool, optional): Only return unresolved spikes
- Returns: Formatted text with spike details

### get_element_feedback
Get feedback for a specific CSS selector.
- `selector` (String, required): CSS selector to look up
- `page` (String, optional): Filter by page name
- Returns: Formatted text with matching element feedback

### get_hotspots
Find elements with the most feedback.
- `limit` (u64, optional): Maximum number of hotspots to return (default: 10)
- Returns: Ranked list of selectors by feedback count

### submit_spike
Create new feedback.
- `page` (String, required): Page name (e.g., 'index.html')
- `comments` (String, required): Feedback comments
- `url` (String, optional): URL of the page
- `selector` (String, optional): CSS selector for element feedback
- `element_text` (String, optional): Text content of the element
- `rating` (String, optional): Rating: love, like, meh, or no
- `reviewer_name` (String, optional): Reviewer name
- `project_key` (String, optional): Project key for the feedback
- Returns: Confirmation with spike ID

### resolve_spike
Mark a spike as resolved.
- `spike_id` (String, required): Spike ID or prefix (minimum 4 characters)
- Returns: Confirmation with resolved timestamp

### delete_spike
Permanently remove a spike.
- `spike_id` (String, required): Spike ID or prefix (minimum 4 characters)
- Returns: Confirmation of deletion

### create_share
Upload a directory and get a shareable URL.
- `directory` (String, required): Directory path to upload
- `name` (String, optional): Name/slug for the share
- `password` (String, optional): Password protection
- Returns: Share URL, slug, and file count

### list_shares
List all your shares.
- No parameters
- Returns: Share metadata (slug, URL, spike count, creation date)

### get_usage
View usage statistics.
- No parameters
- Returns: Spike/share counts, limits, tier, and cost info (agent tier)

## Agent Quickstart

1. **Install the CLI**
   ```bash
   curl -fsSL https://spikes.sh/install.sh | sh
   ```

2. **Create an API key**
   ```bash
   spikes auth create-key --name "my-agent"
   ```

3. **Configure MCP**

   Claude Desktop (`~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`):
   ```json
   {
     "mcpServers": {
       "spikes": {
         "command": "spikes",
         "args": ["mcp", "serve"]
       }
     }
   }
   ```

   Cursor (`.cursor/mcp.json` in your project root):
   ```json
   {
     "mcpServers": {
       "spikes": {
         "command": "spikes",
         "args": ["mcp", "serve"]
       }
     }
   }
   ```

4. **First query** — ask your agent:
   > "Use the get_spikes tool to show me all feedback"

## Data Format

```typescript
interface Spike {
  id: string;                    // nanoid
  type: "page" | "element";
  selector?: string;             // CSS selector for element spikes
  rating: "love" | "like" | "meh" | "no" | null;
  comments: string;
  reviewer: { id: string; name: string };
  timestamp: string;             // ISO 8601
  page: string;
  viewport: { width, height };
}
```

## Installation

```bash
# Install CLI
curl -fsSL https://spikes.sh/install.sh | sh

# Or via Cargo
cargo install spikes
```

## Widget Configuration

```html
<script 
  src="https://spikes.sh/widget.js"
  data-project="my-project"
  data-position="bottom-right"
  data-endpoint="https://my-backend.workers.dev/spikes"
></script>
```

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/bierlingm/spikes): Source code, issues, contributing
- [Landing Page](https://spikes.sh): Marketing site with use cases and pricing
